title: Anthropic Isn't Exaggerating About an AI Panopticon
description: In the debate about the military’s use of artificial intelligence, prompted by Anthropic’s dispute with the Pentagon that’s now headed to the courts, much has been said about the concerns related to autonomous killing. Less examined has been the AI company’s second point of contention: how AI might be used to conduct mass surveillance on Americans. A recent study offers a glimpse at the root of the company’s well-justified trepidation.

title: Top 5 Short Term Courses for Class 12 Science Students
description: After completing Class 12 in the science stream, students can explore several short-term courses to build practical skills and broaden their career opportunities. Options such as clinical research, artificial intelligence and machine learning, robotics and IoT, renewable energy and sustainability, and data analytics provide valuable industry exposure. These courses help students develop technical knowledge, enhance employability, and prepare for careers in healthcare, technology, engineering, environmental science, and data-driven industries., Education, Times Now
content: A clinical research diploma provides science students after class 12 with a chance to learn how new medicines and treatments are trialled prior to their availability to patients. The course normally includes the development of drugs, medical testing ... [2631 chars]

title: After OpenAI, Google-Pentagon partnership lets civilian and military staff create AI agents with Gemini
description: A debate over artificial intelligence in defence has intensified after Anthropic sued the administration of Donald Trump, while Google announced new AI tools for the United States Department of Defense website to enhance search, content management, and cybersecurity.

title: Delhi Technological University, TimesPro announce the inaugural Advanced Certificate Program in Artificial Intelligence
description: New Delhi [India], March 12: As artificial intelligence rapidly reshapes how organisations build products, manage risk, serve customers and run operations, the need for professionals who can design, deploy and govern intelligent systems has accelerated. Delhi Technological University (DTU, New Delhi), in collaboration with TimesPro, has announced the inaugural batch of the Advanced Certificate Programme in Artificial Intelligence, aimed at learners seeking strong conceptual grounding alongside practical, job-relevant capability.
